narrative_summary:
The BRCA2 missense change p.Val2969Met (c.8905G>A) occurs in the DNA‑binding domain of the protein and has been evaluated in several studies for its relevance to hereditary breast‑ovarian cancer risk.  

No definitive clinical segregation or family‑history data are reported for this variant; however, a structural modelling study classified it among benign controls based on protein‑folding analyses and the variant was incorporated into an ACMG/AMP framework that assigned a likely‑benign status, consistent with existing ClinVar assertions that rely on multifactorial scoring [PMC8191791][PMC9433957].  

Functional assays provide conflicting results.  In a drug‑sensitivity screen using BRCA2‑deficient cells, V2969Met behaved as a non‑functional allele, showing consistent sensitivity to multiple PARP inhibitors and was interpreted as supporting pathogenicity [PMC8008494].  Conversely, an HDR‑based functional assay incorporated into an ACMG/AMP classification scheme yielded a HDR score of 3.01 and led to a reclassification of the variant as likely benign [PMC9433957].  

Population data from a Slovenian cohort identified V2969Met in 0.5 % of cancer probands (2/380) and in none of the 80 healthy controls, but the clinical significance of this observation was deemed unknown [PMC3025939].  Computational analyses from a Polish breast/ovarian cancer series reported the variant as novel with modest in‑silico scores, and separate structural modelling indicated that, despite being buried, the substitution is predicted to be stabilising or neutral for protein folding [PMC4429836][PMC8191791].  

Overall, the evidence for BRCA2 V2969Met is discordant: functional drug‑sensitivity data suggest a deleterious effect, whereas HDR functional results, ACMG/AMP modelling, and computational/structural assessments support a benign interpretation.  Given the lack of definitive clinical or segregation data and the mixed functional findings, the variant remains of uncertain significance, with a slight tilt toward a likely‑benign classification pending further corroborative studies.

mentions.quoted_text: The HDR functional dataset was completely concordant with three other functional studies that evaluated the ability for a human BRCA2 variant to restore survival of Brca2 null mouse embryonic stem cells (Table S1). In another functional study, BRCA2 variants were evaluated for influence on sensitivity to PARP inhibitors in a BRCA2-deficient cell line. Among the 63 variants that overlapped with the current HDR functional study, all variants that were non-functional by HDR also demonstrated sensitivity to one or more drugs (non-functional-fClass 4 or fClass 5) or were inconclusive (fClass 3). None were considered resistant (functional-fClass 1 or fClass 2) for any of the four drugs in the sensitivity assays. However, among the variants that were functional by HDR assay, six had non-functional scores (fClass 4 or fClass 5) for at least one drug in the drug sensitivity assay (Table S1, red text). Cells carrying variants p.Val2908Gly (c.8723T>G) and **p.Val2969Met** (**c.8905G>A**) were consistently sensitive (non-functional) across all four drugs, but both of these variants have non-conflicting LB assertions in ClinVar on the basis of a multifactorial score influenced by both family history and co-segregation scores, suggesting that further calibration of the drug sensitivity assay is needed.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article reports that BRCA2 p.Val2969Met shows non‑functional behavior in drug‑sensitivity assays, supporting a pathogenic interpretation despite existing ClinVar likely‑benign classifications.
article_mentions.title: Strong functional data for pathogenicity or neutrality classify BRCA2 DNA-binding-domain variants of uncertain significance

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study reports the BRCA2 V2969M missense variant in 0.5% of Slovenian cancer probands, absent in healthy controls, but its clinical significance is unknown.
article_mentions.title: The occurrence of germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 sequence alterations in Slovenian population

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article reports the discovery of the novel BRCA2 V2969M variant in Polish breast/ovarian cancer patients, presenting only computational prediction data without functional or clinical interpretation.
article_mentions.title: New recurrent BRCA1/2 mutations in Polish patients with familial breast/ovarian cancer detected by next generation sequencing

mentions.quoted_text: To investigate the effect of BRCA2 DBD mutations on protein folding, we computationally modelled their consequences upon incorporation into the three available crystal structures (PDB reference 1MIU, 1MJE, 1IYJ) of rodent Brca2 DBD/Dss1 complexes. We exclusively studied pathogenic mutations affecting evolutionarily conserved residues (W2626C, I2627F, R2659T, E2663V, T2722R, D2723H, D2723G, G2748D and R3052W), since the amino acid sequence of rodent Brca2 DBD is 56% homologous to the human. We compared them to benign variants (K2729N, R2842H, R2888C, **V2969M**, R2973C, V3079I, D3170G) also affecting conserved amino acids. Our analyses (see Materials and Methods) point to two major effects that distinguish pathogenic DBD mutations from benign variants.

A second analysis provides additional evidence for the effects of pathogenic DBD mutations on protein folding. Relative solvent accessibility (RSA) measures how buried an amino acid is relative to its sidechain surface area, with residues that are completely buried (defined as those with an RSA <=0.2) more likely to destabilize protein structure and cause unfolding. All pathogenic mutations are found to be completely buried within the protein structure, and whilst the benign mutations show a range of RSA values, the most buried benign mutations (**V2969M** and V3079I) show stabilizing or negligible changes in DeltaDeltaG.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study compares pathogenic BRCA2 DBD missense mutations to benign controls, noting that V2969M is classified as a benign variant and shows stabilizing structural properties in computational analyses, suggesting it does not disrupt BRCA2 function.
article_mentions.title: Cancer-causing BRCA2 missense mutations disrupt an intracellular protein assembly mechanism to disable genome maintenance

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study incorporated HDR functional assay results into an ACMG/AMP framework and reclassified the BRCA2 V2969M variant as likely benign (LBEN).',
article_mentions.title: Classification of BRCA2 variants of uncertain significance (VUS) using an ACMG/AMP model incorporating a homology directed repair (HDR) functional assay
